title: Fix Duplicate Email Constraint Error on User Invitation
story: As a DataTools Pro user accepting an invitation, I want the system to handle my existing account gracefully, so that I can join a shared account without encountering a database error.
description:
Bug fix for a duplicate entry constraint violation when an invited user who already exists in the system tries to accept an invitation. The invite flow attempted to INSERT a new user record instead of recognizing the existing user, causing a MySQL integrity constraint error on the unique email column.

A secondary error was also discovered: a missing route definition (`teams.create`) caused a 500 error when the invite URL was used after the initial fix. Both issues were resolved.
acceptance_criteria: Invited user who already has an account can accept an invitation without a duplicate entry error
System checks for existing user by email before attempting to create a new record
Invitation acceptance flow completes successfully for both new and existing users
No missing route errors during the invitation acceptance process
middleware_changes: Updated the invitation acceptance flow to check for existing user records before attempting an INSERT, preventing duplicate entry constraint violations. Fixed a missing route definition.
implementation_notes: The root cause was the user creation logic not checking for existing users by email before INSERT. A secondary issue was a missing teams.create route definition in the Teamwork views.
comment_summary: Initial fix resolved the duplicate entry error, but a secondary missing route error appeared. Developer re-tested thoroughly and fixed both issues.
